briefs.title: Council approved discharge of outdated restrictive covenants on private properties
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to approve the discharge of outdated restrictive covenants that the City had previously imposed on private properties. The City Solicitor is now authorized to remove these old restrictions when property owners request it.
briefs.action_required: Contact the City Solicitor if you own a property with an outdated City-imposed restrictive covenant.
briefs.source_body:
7.1 REQUEST TO DISCHARGE CITY-IMPOSED RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS ON PRIVATE PROPERTIES 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council approval for the discharge of outdated restrictive covenants imposed by the City on private properties. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 082 
THAT the proposed discharge of restrictive covenants, as described in Section 3.0 of the report titled “Request to Discharge City-Imposed Restrictive Covenants on Private Properties” dated February 24, 2026, be approved; and 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to discharge the restrictive covenants upon request. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved plumbing permit fee changes in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved changes to plumbing permit fees. The new fees are part of an update to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. The City Solicitor has been authorized to bring forward the official bylaw to put these changes into effect.
briefs.action_required: Check the new plumbing permit fee schedule before your next application.
briefs.source_body:
7.2 BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - PLUMBING PERMIT FEES AMENDMENT 
The Deputy Chief Administrative Officer/Chief Financial Officer submitted a report proposing minor amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 

Resolution No. 2026 - 083 
THAT the proposed changes to fees and charges, as outlined in the report titled “Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw – Plumbing Permit Fee Amendment”, dated February 24, 2026, be approved, and 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a bylaw to amend the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to implement the provisions as set out in the report. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby North Road BIA renewal for 2026–2031
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved the renewal of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for the 2026–2031 term. Council authorized staff to work with the BIA on the renewal and directed the City Solicitor to prepare the necessary bylaw. A BIA is a zone where local businesses pay a special levy to fund improvements and marketing for the area.
briefs.action_required: No action required — the renewal is already approved and proceeding.
briefs.source_body:
7.3 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BUSINESS IMPROVEMENT AREA (BIA) RENEWAL 
The Deputy Chief Administrative Officer/Chief Financial Officer submitted a report seeking Council’s approval to proceed with the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA) request to renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area for the period 2026-2031. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 084 
THAT staff be authorized to work with the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association towards the renewal of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area, as outlined in the report titled “Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Renewal”, dated February 24,2026. 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, substantially as set out in Attachment 4 to the report; and 
THAT a copy of this report be sent to the Executive Director, Burnaby North Road BIA. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved new rules for selling bear spray in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to amend the Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the sale of bear spray. The new rules require businesses to follow specific conditions when selling bear spray, and the city can now enforce those rules through fines or other penalties.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells bear spray and review the new bylaw rules.
briefs.source_body:
7.4 REGULATING THE SALE OF BEAR SPRAY 
The General Manager Community Safety submitted a report seeking Council approval to bring forward proposed amendments to the Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the sale of bear spray in Burnaby. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 

Resolution No. 2026 - 085 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017, substantially as set out in Attachment 1 to the report titled “Regulating the Sale of Bear Spray” dated February 24, 2026; and 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, substantially as set out in Section 3.3 of the report. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved hazard study for Lower Westridge, new building permits paused
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to start a Natural Hazard Development Permit Area program and a hazard assessment for the Lower Westridge neighbourhood. Council also approved $120,000 from the Gaming Reserve for the assessment and will ask the BC government to pause new construction permits in Lower Westridge until the study is done.
briefs.action_required: Check if your Lower Westridge property is affected by the building permit pause.
briefs.source_body:
7.5 NATURAL HAZARD DEVELOPMENT PERMIT AREA AND GUIDELINES AND LOWER WESTRIDGE ASSESSMENT - PROJECT INITIATION 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council direction regarding the development of a Natural Hazard Development Permit Area (DPA) and Guidelines program for Burnaby, and approval of a new budget item to advance a hazard assessment of the Lower Westridge neighbourhood. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 086 
THAT staff be directed to initiate the City-Wide Natural Hazard Development Permit Area and Guidelines project; 
THAT staff be directed to initiate the Lower Westridge Hazard Assessment to determine whether properties in the Lower Westridge area may be exempt from Small Scale Multi-Unit Housing mandates due to a hazardous condition; and 
THAT additional funding of $120,000 from the Gaming Reserve be approved to fund the Lower Westridge Hazard Assessment. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 

Arising from discussion, the Mayor introduced the following motion: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 087 
THAT the Mayor, on behalf of Council, send a letter to the BC Minister of Housing and Municipal Affairs, requesting that the City of Burnaby be granted permission to not allow processing of new construction permits in the Lower Westridge area, until the Lower Westridge Hazard Assessment is completed. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved a statutory right-of-way for Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre utilities
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved granting a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission to use a portion of land) over part of 3860 Sperling Avenue to utility providers. This allows them to install and maintain infrastructure needed for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project.
briefs.source_body:
7.6 STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY - 3860 SPERLING AVENUE (BURNABY LAKE RECREATION CENTRE) 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council approval to grant a Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 3860 Sperling Avenue to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of works. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 088 
THAT the granting and execution of any required Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 3860 Sperling Avenue in favour of third-party utility providers for the installation of infrastructure necessary to service the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project, as outlined in the report titled “Statutory Right-of-Way – 3860 Sperling Avenue, dated February 24, 2026, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council referred antennae variance permit back to staff for review
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to refer Development Variance Permit (DVP) #25-01 back to staff for further review. The permit would have allowed antennae equipment at 8888 University Drive to exceed normal rules for building face coverage and height. Council decided more work is needed before making a final decision.
briefs.source_body:
7.7 DVP #25-01 - 8888 UNIVERSITY DRIVE - VARIANCES TO BUILDING FACE COVERAGE AND HEIGHT FOR EXISTING AND FUTURE ANTENNAE EQUIPMENT 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #25-01. 
Arising from discussion, the original recommendation, as noted in the report, was not brought forward, and the following motion was introduced: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 089 
THAT the report titled "DVP #25-01 - 8888 University Drive – Variances to Building Face Coverage and Height for Existing and Future Antennae Equipment", dated February 24, 2026, be REFERRED back to staff for further review. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council referred antennae variance request back to staff for review
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to refer Development Variance Permit #25-02 back to staff for further review. The permit would have allowed variances to building face coverage and height rules for existing and future antennae equipment at 9001 University Crescent. No decision was made on the variances themselves.
briefs.source_body:
7.8 DVP #25-02 - 9001 UNIVERSITY CRES - VARIANCES TO BUILDING FACE COVERAGE AND HEIGHT FOR EXISTING AND FUTURE ANTENNAE EQUIPMENT 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #25-02. 
Arising from discussion, the original recommendation, as noted in the report, was not brought forward, and the following motion was introduced: 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 090 
THAT the report titled “DVP #25-02 - 9001 University Cres – Variances to Building Face Coverage and Height for Existing and Future Antennae Equipment”, dated February 24, 2026, be REFERRED back to staff for further review. 

CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved renewal of fleet vehicle parking permit at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a five-year renewal of Temporary Use Permit (TUP) 26-01 for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway. A Temporary Use Permit allows a specific land use for a limited time. Council also directed the City Solicitor to register the permit with the Land Title Office.
briefs.source_body:
7.9 TUP #26-01 (RENEWAL) - 7000 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council approval for Temporary Use Permit (TUP) 26-01 renewal for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 091 
THAT Temporary Use Permit #26-01, included as Attachment 2 to the report titled “TUP #26-01 (Renewal) – 7000 Lougheed Highway” dated February 24, 2026, be approved, and 
THAT the City Solicitor be directed to register notice of Temporary Use Permit #26-01 with the Land Title Office. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council suspended $63K FCM dues until Burnaby gets caucus seat
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to suspend payment of $63,005.33 in Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) membership dues for 2026-2027. The payment will not be made until the City of Burnaby receives a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus.
briefs.source_body:
7.10 2026-2027 FCM MEMBERSHIP DUES 
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council approval for payment of the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) 2026-2027 membership dues. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 

THAT payment of the invoice from the Federation of Canadian Municipalities for the 2026-2027 membership dues in the amount of $63,005.33, including taxes, be approved. 
                       
AMENDED  
Arising from discussion, the Mayor introduced the following amendment to the original motion: 

MOVED AND SECONDED 

THAT payment of the invoice from the Federation of Canadian Municipalities for the 2026-2027 membership dues in the amount of $63,005.33, including taxes, be suspended until the City of Burnaby receives a place on the Big City Mayors' Caucus. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 

The motion, AS AMENDED, was than put to a final vote: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 092 
THAT payment of the invoice from the Federation of Canadian Municipalities for the 2026-2027 membership dues in the amount of $63,005.33, including taxes, be suspended until the City of Burnaby receives a place on the Big City Mayors' Caucus. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Option B for the Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously approved Option B for the Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative. This decision finalizes the design and location for the new community landmark sign, as outlined in a staff report from February 24, 2026.
briefs.source_body:
7.11 LANDMARK BURNABY SIGN 
The General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture submitted a report providing Council with an update on the Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative following Council’s previous direction.  

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 093 
THAT staff be authorized to proceed with Option B, as outlined in the report titled “Landmark Burnaby Sign”, dated February 24, 2026. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved up to 84 special outdoor events for 2026 at three parks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved up to 84 special outdoor events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium between March 15 and November 1, 2026. The approval was granted under the Burnaby Noise or Sound Abatement Bylaw, which controls noise levels at public events. The decision was unanimous.

key_dates.label: Special outdoor events season begins — Burnaby
Special outdoor events season ends — Burnaby

briefs.source_body:
7.12 AUTHORIZATION FOR SPECIAL OUTDOOR EVENTS 2026 
The General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture submitted a report seeking Council authorization, under the Burnaby Noise or Sound Abatement Bylaw, for outdoor events at specific locations. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 094 
THAT approval be given for up to 84 events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park and Swangard Stadium between March 15, 2026, and November 1, 2026, as outlined in the report titled “Authorization for Special Outdoor Events 2026, dated February 24, 2026. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $162,400 contract renewal for portable rental toilets
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. for portable rental toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes. The final payment will be based on actual quantities delivered and unit rates submitted.
briefs.source_body:
7.13 CR - PORTABLE RENTAL TOILETS 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Portable Rental Toilets. 
*Councillor Tetrault left at 6:27 pm  
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 095 
THAT a contract renewal to Jiffy John Rentals Ltd., for an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Portable Rental Toilets”, dated February 24, 2026, be approved; and   
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of rentals delivered and unit rates submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $670,000 contract renewal for security officer services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a one-year contract renewal with Guardteck Security Corp for security officer services at city locations and special events. The contract is valued at an estimated $670,000 including taxes, with final payment based on actual hours worked and agreed hourly rates.
briefs.source_body:
7.14 CR - SECURITY OFFICER SERVICES 
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Security Officer Services at various City locations and Special Events.  
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 096 
THAT a one (1) year contract renewal to Guardteck Security Corp for an estimated cost of $670,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Security Officer Services” dated February 24, 2026, be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantity of services delivered and hourly rates submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved heritage alteration permit for W.J. Mathers House
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit for the W.J. Mathers House "Altnadene" at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue. The permit allows changes to the building's chimneys. A Heritage Alteration Permit is a special permission needed to make changes to a protected historic property.
briefs.source_body:
8.1 CHC - HAP FOR 6490 DEER LAKE AVENUE 
The Community Heritage Commission (CHC) submitted a report seeking Council approval for the issuance of a Heritage Alteration Permit (HAP) for proposed alterations to the chimneys at the W.J. Mathers House “Altnadene”, located at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 097 
THAT the issuance of a Heritage Alteration Permit for the W.J. Mathers House "Altnadene", as outlined in the report titled "HAP for 6490 Deer Lake Avenue" dated February 12, 2026, of the Community Heritage Commission meeting, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved on-street parking fee rate increase in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved an increase to on-street public parking meter fees. The new rates, detailed in a February 17, 2026 report, are now in effect. This means drivers will pay more to park at metered spots on city streets.
briefs.action_required: Check the new parking meter rates before your next use.
briefs.source_body:
8.2 FMC - ON-STREET PUBLIC PAY PARKING UPDATE 
The Financial Management Committee (CHC) submitted a report providing Council information regarding public pay parking meter spaces and to seek Council approval for the proposed on-street public parking fee rate increase. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 098 
THAT the proposed parking fee rate detailed in Section 3.3 of the report titled "On-Street Public Pay Parking Update" dated February 17, 2026, of the Financial Management Committee meeting, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved energy-based user fees for public EV charging stations
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved a new energy-based user fee system for city-owned public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The decision, passed unanimously, replaces the previous time-based fees with charges based on the amount of electricity used. Staff have also been authorized to apply for a temporary exemption from Measurement Canada to sell electricity for EV charging, and the City Solicitor will bring forward bylaw amendments to implement the new fees.
briefs.action_required: No immediate action required; monitor for bylaw amendment updates.
briefs.source_body:
8.3 FMC - PUBLIC ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGING NETWORK - ENERGY-BASED USER FEE 
The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report proposing new energy-based user fees system for City-owned public electric vehicle charging stations. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 099 
THAT the proposed energy-based user fees, as detailed in the report titled “Public Electric Vehicle Charging Network – Energy-Based User Fees” dated February 17, 2026, of the Financial Management Committee, be approved for City-owned public electric vehicle charging stations. 
THAT staff be authorized to apply for Temporary Dispensation from Measurement Canada for the sale of electricity for electric vehicle charging; and 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the following bylaws to implement energy-based fees for electric vehicle charging: 
• Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019 
• Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw 
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 
in accordance with Attachment 1 of the report. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 20-storey mixed-use building at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-13) for a 20-storey mixed-use rental development at 5058 Imperial Street. The project will include a double-height commercial storefront at street level and replaces a previously approved six-storey rental building on the same site.
briefs.source_body:
8.4 PDC - REZ #25-13 - 5058 IMPERIAL STREET - MIXED USE DEVELOPMENT 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-13 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment is to permit the construction of a 20-storey mixed-use multiple-family market rental residential development fronting Imperial Street with a double-height commercial storey at street level. The proposed development concept supersedes a previously approved plan for a six-storey market and non-market rental apartment building on the site approved through REZ #20-36. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 100 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-13 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report titled “REZ #25-13 – 5058 Imperial Street – Mixed Use Development”, dated February 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-13; and 
THAT upon Council granting Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-13, the City Solicitor be directed to bring forward a bylaw to repeal Burnaby Housing Agreement (5058 Imperial Street) Bylaw 2023, and to discharge Covenant CB1022856, as set out in Section 3.3 of the report. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for Blake Village master plan update
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-18) for 6800 Lougheed Highway. The bylaw updates the Blake Village Conceptual Master Plan to match the new OCP 2050 height rules and adds more open space. No new buildings or subdivisions are proposed at this stage.
briefs.source_body:
8.5 PDC - REZ #25-18 - 6800 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY - BLAKE VILLAGE CONCEPTUAL MASTER PLAN UPDATE 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-18 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the zoning bylaw amendment is to advance a Master Plan (MP) amendment to the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual MP to establish a revised site plan with updated height and massing in alignment with the recently adopted OCP 2050 and associated heigh-based zoning districts (R6 and CM1), as well as expanded open space provisions for the site. No development or subdivision is proposed through the subject MP amendment. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 101 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-18 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT the Parks and Open Space strategy, as outlined in Section 3.10 of the report titled “REZ #25-18 – 6800 Lougheed Highway – BLAKE Village Conceptual Master Plan Update”, dated February 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, be endorsed; and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 of the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-18. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 49-storey tower at Springer Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted to advance a rezoning bylaw that allows a 49-storey apartment tower with ground-floor retail at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The decision also authorizes a housing agreement and the sale of city-owned land for the project, subject to final approval.
briefs.source_body:
8.6 PDC - REZ #25-20 - 2209 AND 2225 SPRINGER AVENUE - MIXED USE MULTIPLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT WITH COMMERCIAL 
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-20 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the zoning bylaw amendment is to permit the construction of a single high-rise apartment tower of up to 49 storeys inclusive of 9 storey terraced podium with street fronting retail. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 102 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-20 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT a Housing Agreement be authorized if applicable according to the terms outlined in Section 3.5 of the report titled “REZ #25-20 – 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue – Mixed Use Multiple Family Residential Development with Commercial,” dated February 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; 
THAT the sale be approved in principle of City owned property for inclusion within the subject development site in accordance with the terms outlined in Section 3.7 of the report, and subject to the applicant pursuing the rezoning proposal to completion; and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-20. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved active transportation education initiatives for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved active transportation education and promotion initiatives for 2026. The approved programs are detailed in Section 3.3 of the Transportation Committee's February 11, 2026 report.
briefs.source_body:
8.7 TC - 2026 ACTIVE TRANSPORTATION EDUCATION AND PROMOTION 
The Transportation Committee (TC) submitted a report seeking Council approval for active transportation education and promotion initiatives in 2026. 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 103 
THAT the initiatives detailed in Section 3.3 of the report titled “2026 Active Transportation Education and Promotion” dated February 11, 2026, of the Transportation Committee meeting, be approved.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council referred Burnaby Football Club request to staff for field strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a referral of the Burnaby Football Club's request to city staff. Staff will include the request in the ongoing work to create a sports field strategy plan. The work will be done in collaboration with community stakeholders, including the Burnaby Football Club.
briefs.source_body:
9.6 PRCC - REFER TO STAFF: BURNABY FOOTBALL CLUB DELEGATION 
The Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee (PRCC) submitted a report seeking referral of the Burnaby Football Club's request to staff for inclusion in the ongoing sports field strategy plan work. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 – 114 
THAT the delegation from Burnaby Football Club received at the January 28, 2026, Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee meeting, be REFERRED to staff for inclusion in the ongoing sports field strategy plan work, in collaboration with community stakeholders, including Burnaby Football Club. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council gave first and second readings to Burnaby zoning text amendment Bylaw No. 14792
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give first and second readings to Bylaw No. 14792, a text amendment to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965. This means the bylaw has passed two early stages of approval and will move forward in the process.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14792 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 - Text Amendment 

 MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 115-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14792 be now read a first and second time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved first two readings for Buller Avenue rezoning
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give first and second reading to Bylaw No. 14794, which rezones 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue. This is an early step in the approval process; the bylaw still needs third reading and adoption before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — monitor for public hearing dates.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14794 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 - REZ #25-07 (7305 & 7317 Buller Ave.) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 115-2 
THAT Bylaw No. 14794 be now read a first and second time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given first, second, and third reading to Bylaw No. 14797, which renews the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for 2026. A BIA is a defined area where local businesses pay a special levy to fund improvements and marketing for the neighbourhood. The bylaw was passed unanimously.
briefs.action_required: Check your property tax notice for the BIA levy amount if you are a North Road business owner.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026  

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 116-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14797 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given three readings to an amendment to the Business Licence Bylaw. This means the bylaw has been formally approved and is now law. The amendment updates the rules for business licences in the city.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2 #14798 - Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2025  
MOVED AND SECONDED 

Resolution No. 2025 - 116-2 
THAT Bylaw No. 14798 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby council approved bylaw notice enforcement rule changes
briefs.summary: Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2026, Amendment Bylaw No. 1. This means the bylaw has been approved and will update how bylaw notices are enforced in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3 #14799 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 116-3 
THAT Bylaw No. 14799 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby council approved fee and charge bylaw amendments
briefs.summary: Council gave three readings to Bylaw No. 14800, which amends the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This means the bylaw has been approved and will update various municipal fees and charges in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4 #14800 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 

MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 116-4 
THAT Bylaw No. 14800 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby Procedure Bylaw amendment on first, second, and third reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give first, second, and third reading to Bylaw No. 14801, which amends the city's Procedure Bylaw. This means the bylaw has been approved at the council level and will now move forward to adoption.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.5 #14801 - Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 116-5 
THAT Bylaw No. 14801 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 10th Avenue properties
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave final approval to Bylaw No. 14780, rezoning several properties on 10th Avenue. The bylaw was adopted unanimously, meaning the zoning change is now in effect for the addresses listed.
briefs.source_body:
11.3.1 #14780 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 17, 2025 - REZ #25-08 (8265/69/73/77/81/87/89/93 and 8303 10th Avenue) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 117 
THAT Bylaw No. 14780 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council received petition against drug-permitted shelter at Gilmore Diversion
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received a petition from the WeVote Burnaby Gilmore Shelter Watch group regarding a proposed drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion. Council voted unanimously to accept the petition for information only, meaning no action was taken on the petition itself.
briefs.source_body:
12.1 PETITION: Proposed Drug-Permitted Shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion 
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report providing Council with a petition from the WeVote Burnaby Gilmore Shelter Watch regarding the proposed drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion, Burnaby. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 118 
THAT the attached Petition from the WeVote Burnaby Gilmore Shelter Watch regarding the proposed drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion, Burnaby, received by Council on February 24, 2026, be received for information. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved Good Neighbour Plan requirement for Gilmore shelter
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to require BC Housing and the shelter operator to submit a Good Neighbour Plan and an Operational Management Plan before the 3020 Gilmore Diversion shelter can open. Council also recommended the operators re-engage with the public. The plans must address safety, crime, and neighbourhood concerns.
briefs.source_body:
13.1 PROPOSED MOTION: Councillor Lee - 3020 Gilmore Diversion BC Housing Project 
Councillor Lee introduced a Proposed Motion seeking Council approval to proactively address issues related to service provisions, community integration, and effective allocation of City resources associated with shelter operations at 3020 Gilmore Diversion: 
... 
The motion, AS AMENDED, was then put to a final vote: 

MOVED AND SECONDED 

Resolution No. 2026 – 119 
WHEREAS BC Housing had purchased the two-storey office building at 3020 Gilmore Diversion in Burnaby in March 2025 and is planning to create a new permanent shelter at this site; 
WHEREAS according to the 2025 Homeless Count Data the majority of unsheltered individuals did not identify addiction nor substance use as the primary cause of homelessness; 
WHEREAS a petition with nearly 4000 signatures opposing a shelter at this site has been delivered to the City of Burnaby and BC Housing; 
WHEREAS the petitioners are concerned that since this shelter may accept active drug users it may bring serious safety, crime, and public-disorder risks to the neighbourhood; 
WHEREAS shelter operations may generate off-site impacts that increase demand on municipal services; and 
WHEREAS Council has a responsibility to manage public safety, neighbourhood livability, and the effective use of City resources; 
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT  
1. prior to occupancy approval of the 3020 Gilmore Shelter, Council requests that BC Housing and the project operator submit, to the satisfaction and approval of the City, both: 
a) a Good Neighbour Plan; and 
b) an Operational Management Plan, 
developed with appropriate consultation of surrounding residents and community stakeholders, addressing measures to mitigate off-site impacts and anticipate demands on municipal services; and 

2. THAT Council recommend to BC Housing and Lookout Society to more comprehensively re-engage with the public as soon as practicable. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
